source.section: 2.1 - Air-to-Air Modes > 2.1.8 - Radar Lock Acquisition > 2.1.8.3 - JHMCS (Joint Helmet-Cuesing System) Lock

steps.action: Turn HMD Brightness Control Switch to BRT
steps.voiced: Turn HMD Brightness Control Switch to BRT.

steps.action: The HMD (Helmet-Mounted Display) overlay will appear over your helmet (except when looking at the Heads-Up Display). When the HMD is looking at the HUD, it scans identically to Boresight Acquisition. When the HMD is pointed elsewhere, it maintains the scan shape but is slaved to the HMD center. When the radar scan limits are reached, the dashed circle will not move past the limits and will flash if the HMD moves farther than it can.
steps.voiced: Confirm HMD overlay is visible — scan is slaved to helmet center and constrained by radar limits.

steps.action: Press Sensor Control Switch Forward to enter desired acquisition mode Holding it for more than 0.8 sec selects LHACQ (Long Range Helmet Acquisition) mode, which locks targets automatically within 40 nm of the helmet reticle Holding it for less than 0.8 sec selects HACQ (Helmet Acquisition) mode, which locks targets automatically within 10 nm of the helmet reticle
steps.voiced: Press and hold Sensor Control Switch Forward — over 0.8 seconds for LHACQ out to 40 nm, under 0.8 seconds for HACQ out to 10 nm.

steps.action: A STT (Single Target Track) lock will be performed automatically when target comes on target reticle and is within acquisition range To exit ACM mode, press Undesignate Button.
steps.voiced: Radar auto-locks when target enters the reticle — press Undesignate Button to exit.
